page.content: In the period from 2019 to 2024, the grain storage capacity is detailed by year and grain type, beginning with 2019, which recorded a wheat volume of 500,000 tons, corn at 1,200,000 tons, barley at 300,000 tons, and oats at 200,000 tons, totaling 2,100,000 tons with a utilization rate of 85%. In 2020, the capacity increased to a wheat volume of 520,000 tons, corn at 1,250,000 tons, barley at 310,000 tons, and oats at 210,000 tons, totaling 2,170,000 tons with a utilization rate of 87%. The year 2021 saw further increases with wheat at 540,000 tons, corn at 1,300,000 tons, barley at 320,000 tons, and oats at 220,000 tons, bringing total capacity to 2,250,000 tons and a utilization rate of 90%. In 2022, wheat reached 550,000 tons, corn 1,350,000 tons, barley 330,000 tons, and oats 230,000 tons, resulting in a total of 2,280,000 tons with a utilization rate of 92%.

recorded a wheat volume of 500,000 tons, corn at 1,200,000 tons, barley at 300,000 tons, and oats at 200,000 tons, totaling
2,100,000 tons with a utilization rate of 85%. In 2020, the capacity increased to a wheat volume of 520,000 tons, corn at
1,250,000 tons, barley at 310,000 tons, and oats at 210,000 tons, totaling 2,170,000 tons with a utilization rate of 87%. The year
2021 saw further increases with wheat at 540,000 tons, corn at 1,300,000 tons, barley at 320,000 tons, and oats at 220,000 tons,
bringing total capacity to 2,250,000 tons and a utilization rate of 90%. In 2022, wheat reached 550,000 tons, corn 1,350,000
tons, barley 330,000 tons, and oats 230,000 tons, resulting in a total of 2,280,000 tons with a utilization rate of 92%.

page.content: The year 2023 reported wheat at 570,000 tons, corn at 1,400,000 tons, barley at 340,000 tons, and oats at 240,000 tons, totaling 2,320,000 tons and a utilization rate of 93%. Finally, in 2024, wheat is projected at 600,000 tons, corn at 1,450,000 tons, barley at 350,000 tons, and oats at 250,000 tons, culminating in a total capacity of 2,400,000 tons with a utilization rate of 95%. The total capacity across these years is assessed through various metrics, including yearly change and growth rate, providing a comprehensive overview of grain storage trends, demand forecasts, and market insights essential for strategic initiatives, operational efficiency, risk management, and investment strategies in the agricultural sector.
